_meta.cpu: AMD Ryzen Threadripper PRO 5955WX (16 cores / 32 threads)
_meta.bench: llama-bench -p 512 -n 64 (prefill/decode tok/s); llama-perplexity wikitext-2-raw test, 8 chunks, n_ctx 512
_meta.honest_note: 7B = the FAIR case for sasori. Even here, on perplexity-per-bit, the mature llama.cpp K-quant Q4_K_M DOMINATES sasori (smaller 4.68 vs 7.23GB, better PPL 7.12 vs 7.77, faster). sasori-TQ3P only beats the 8-bit Q8_0 (weak win). sasori has NO efficiency/quality-per-bit advantage as a deployment kernel. CAVEAT: PPL != task accuracy (the Papers B/C theme); sasori-K3 retains ~98% GSM8K for this model (paper data), but Q4_K_M's task accuracy is not measured here and is likely also competitive. The genuine sasori finding here is the SCALE effect: TQ2P PPL/F16 recovers 10.5x (1.7B) -> 3.3x (7B), cross-validating Paper C in the deployed kernel.
